1.1: Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, according to the promise of life that is in Christ Jesus,
1.2: To Timothy, my beloved child: Grace, mercy, and peace from God the Father and Christ Jesus our Lord.
1.3: I have gratitude to God, whom I serve from the forefathers with a pure conscience, as I have unceasing remembrance of you in my prayers night and day,
1.4: longing to see you, recalling your tears, so that I may be filled with joy,
1.5: having taken remembrance of the unfeigned faith in you, which dwelt first in your grandmother Lois and in your mother Eunice, and I am persuaded that it is in you also.
1.6: Because of this reason, I remind you to rekindle the gift of God, which is in you through the laying on of my hands.
1.7: For God did not give us a spirit of timidity, but of power and love and soundness of mind.
1.8: Therefore you should not be ashamed of the testimony of our Lord, nor of me, His prisoner; but suffer together for the gospel according to the power of God,
1.9: the One having saved us and having called us with a holy calling, not according to our works but according to His own purpose and grace, having been given to us in Christ Jesus before times eternal,
1.10: and now having been made manifest through the appearing of our Savior Christ Jesus, having indeed abolished death and having brought to light life and immortality through the gospel,
1.11: to which I was appointed a preacher and an apostle and a teacher.
1.12: Because of this reason I also suffer these things. But I am not ashamed, for I know whom I have believed, and I am persuaded that He is able to guard my deposit entrusted for that day.
1.13: Hold the pattern of sound words, which you heard from me, in the faith and love that are in Christ Jesus.
1.14: Guard the good deposit entrusted by the Holy Spirit, the One dwelling in us.
1.15: You know this, that all those in Asia turned away from me, of whom are Phygelus and Hermogenes.
1.16: May the Lord give mercy to the household of Onesiphorus, because he refreshed me often, and he was not ashamed of my chain.
1.17: But being in Rome, he earnestly sought me and found me .
1.18: May the Lord give to him to find mercy from the Lord in that day! And you know very well how much he served in Ephesus.

2.1: You, therefore, my child, be empowered in the grace that is in Christ Jesus.
2.2: And the things that you heard from me through many witnesses, entrust these to faithful men such as will be sufficient to teach others also.
2.3: Share in suffering as a good soldier of Christ Jesus.
2.4: No one serving as a soldier entangles himself in the affairs of life, so that he might please the one having enlisted him.
2.5: Now also, if anyone competes, he is not crowned unless he shall have competed lawfully.
2.6: It is necessary for the toiling farmer first to partake of the fruits.
2.7: Consider what I say, for the Lord will give you understanding in all things.
2.8: Remember Jesus Christ, having been raised out from the dead, of the seed of David, according to my gospel,
2.9: in which I suffer hardship even to chains as an evildoer. But the word of God has not been bound!
2.10: Because of this, I endure all things because of the chosen ones, so that they also may obtain the salvation that is in Christ Jesus, with eternal glory.
2.11: Trustworthy is the word: For if we died together with Him , we will also live together with Him ;
2.12: if we endure, we will also reign together with Him ; if we will deny Him , He also will deny us;
2.13: if we are faithless, He remains faithful, for He is not able to deny Himself.
2.14: Remind them these things, earnestly testifying before God not to fight about words, useful for nothing but to the subversion of those hearing.
2.15: Be diligent to present yourself approved to God, a workman unashamed, straightly cutting the word of truth.
2.16: But avoid profane, empty utterances, for they will advance to more ungodliness,
2.17: and their word will have pasture like gangrene, of whom are Hymenaeus and Philetus,
2.18: who missed the mark concerning the truth, saying the resurrection already to have happened; and they are overturning the faith of some.
2.19: Nevertheless, the firm foundation of God has stood, having this seal: “The Lord knows those being His,” and, “Let everyone naming the name of the Lord depart from unrighteousness.”
2.20: Now in a great house there are not only golden and silver vessels, but also wooden and earthen; and some indeed for honor, but some for dishonor.
2.21: Therefore if anyone shall have cleansed himself from these, he will be a vessel for honor, having been sanctified, useful to the Master, having been prepared for every good work.
2.22: Now flee youthful lusts and pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace, along with those calling on the Lord out of a pure heart.
2.23: And refuse foolish and ignorant controversies, knowing that they beget quarrels.
2.24: And it is necessary for a servant of the Lord not to quarrel, but to be kind toward all, able to teach, forbearing,
2.25: in gentleness disciplining those opposing, lest ever God may give them repentance unto a knowledge of the truth,
2.26: and they might come to their senses out of the snare of the devil, having been captured by him unto his will.

3.1: But know this, that in the last days difficult times will be present.
3.2: For men will be lovers of self, lovers of money, boasters, proud, verbally abusive, disobedient to parents, ungrateful, unholy,
3.3: unloving, unforgiving, slanderous, without control, savage, without love of good,
3.4: betrayers, reckless, having been puffed up, lovers of pleasure rather than lovers of God,
3.5: having a form of godliness but having denied its power. And turn away from these.
3.6: For out of these are those slipping into households and taking captive weak women, heaped up with sins, being led by various passions,
3.7: always learning and never being able to come to a knowledge of the truth.
3.8: Now by which way Jannes and Jambres opposed Moses, so also these oppose the truth, men having been corrupted in mind, disqualified concerning the faith.
3.9: But they will not advance much further, for their folly will be evident to all, as also that of those two became.
3.10: But you have closely followed my teaching, conduct, purpose, faith, patience, love, endurance,
3.11: persecutions, sufferings, such as happened to me in Antioch, in Iconium, in Lystra; what manner of persecutions I bore up under! And the Lord delivered me out of all.
3.12: And all those also wanting to live godlily in Christ Jesus will be persecuted.
3.13: But evil men and imposters will advance to the worse, deceiving and being deceived.
3.14: But you, abide in the things you learned and were convinced of, having known from whom you learned,
3.15: and that from infancy you have known the sacred writings, which are able to make you wise unto salvation through faith in Christ Jesus.
3.16: Every Scripture is God-breathed and profitable for teaching, for conviction, for correction, and for training in righteousness,
3.17: so that the man of God may be complete, having been completely equipped toward every good work.

4.1: I earnestly testify before God and Christ Jesus, the One being about to judge the living and the dead, and by His appearing and His kingdom:
4.2: Preach the word; be ready in season and out of season; convict, rebuke, exhort, in all patience and teaching.
4.3: For there will be a season when they will not bear with sound teaching, but according to their own passions, having an itching ear, they will heap up teachers to themselves,
4.4: and indeed they will turn away from hearing the truth, and they will be turned aside to myths.
4.5: But you, be sober in all things, suffer hardships, do the work of an evangelist, fully carry out your ministry.
4.6: For I am already being poured out as a drink offering, and the time of my departure has come.
4.7: I have fought the good fight, I have finished the course, I have kept the faith.
4.8: From now on there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will award to me in that day; but not only to me, but also to all those loving His appearing.
4.9: Be diligent to come to me quickly,
4.10: for Demas deserted me, having loved the present age, and he went to Thessalonica, Crescens to Galatia, and Titus to Dalmatia.
4.11: Luke alone is with me. Having taken Mark, bring him with you, for he is useful to me for the ministry.
4.12: But I sent Tychicus to Ephesus.
4.13: Upon coming, bring the cloak that I left in Troas with Carpus, and the books, especially the parchments.
4.14: Alexander the coppersmith did much harm to me. The Lord will repay to him according to his works,
4.15: of whom you also beware, for exceedingly he opposed our word.
4.16: In my first defense, no one came beside me, but all deserted me. May it not be reckoned to them!
4.17: But the Lord stood by me and empowered me, so that through me the proclamation might be fully carried out, and all the Gentiles might hear. And I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion.
4.18: The Lord will deliver me from every evil work and will bring me safely into His heavenly kingdom, to whom is the glory unto the ages of the ages. Amen.
4.19: Greet Prisca and Aquila, and the house of Onesiphorus.
4.20: Erastus remained in Corinth, but Trophimus I left being sick in Miletus.
4.21: Be diligent to come before winter. Eubulus greets you, and Pudens, and Linus, and Claudia, and all the brothers.
4.22: The Lord be with your spirit. Grace be with you? all.
